understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to complete each level by getting the relevant pushable object(s) onto one or more goal tiles marked with '+', or otherwise triggering a state the engine reports as WIN. Once any level results in WIN, the overall run is considered a success.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement occurred relative to a currently selected piece. The CLICK action selected a piece or teleported the selected piece between certain linked tiles (e.g., between [21, 21] and [42, 21]), suggesting pre-defined portals or paired locations. The MOVE_* actions (MOVE_LEFT, MOVE_RIGHT, MOVE_UP, MOVE_DOWN) attempted to move the selected piece one tile in the specified direction. If the target tile was blocked by a wall, obstacle, boundary, or possibly an immovable configuration of objects, the move failed and the piece did not change position. Pushing likely occurred when moving into a tile containing a pushable object: if the space beyond that object was free, the object would be pushed; if not, the move would be blocked. Selection was controlled by CLICK: clicking on coordinates that corresponded to another valid piece could change which piece was active, or in some cases move the active piece to that location according to the level’s teleport/portal rules.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to complete each level by getting the relevant pushable object(s) onto one or more goal tiles marked with '+', or otherwise triggering a state the engine reports as WIN. Once any level results in WIN, the overall run is considered a success.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement occurred relative to a currently selected piece. The CLICK action selected a piece or teleported the selected piece between certain linked tiles (e.g., between [21, 21] and [42, 21]), suggesting pre-defined portals or paired locations. The MOVE_* actions (MOVE_LEFT, MOVE_RIGHT, MOVE_UP, MOVE_DOWN) attempted to move the selected piece one tile in the specified direction. If the target tile was blocked by a wall, obstacle, boundary, or possibly an immovable configuration of objects, the move failed and the piece did not change position. Pushing likely occurred when moving into a tile containing a pushable object: if the space beyond that object was free, the object would be pushed; if not, the move would be blocked. Selection was controlled by CLICK: clicking on coordinates that corresponded to another valid piece could change which piece was active, or in some cases move the active piece to that location according to the level’s teleport/portal rules.
